The model for total time combines three distinct database functions line.
Each function depends on a specific portion of the total query count line.
MySQL time grows with a quadratic term plus an exponential term line.
Oracle time uses a logarithmic term and a reciprocal fraction term line.
Sybase time involves a power of three halves minus a square root line.
We substitute the proportion variables into each individual response function line.
The total time equals the sum of all three adjusted time functions line.
Minimization requires finding the partial derivatives with respect to alpha and beta line.
Setting these derivatives to zero yields the necessary conditions for optimality line.
Solving the resulting system gives the best distribution of query load line.
With Q equal to one thousand we calculate the specific numeric values line.
The constants provided allow us to compute the exact optimal proportions line.
Numerical methods help find the root of the complex derivative equations line.
The original line shows how the initial model guides the final result line.
